intro: Python's concise, script-friendly syntax expands dramatically when converted to Java's enterprise-grade structure. Python functions become Java class methods, Python's `list` and `dict` map to `ArrayList` and `HashMap`, optional type hints become mandatory type declarations, and Python's `try/except` becomes Java's checked-exception `try/catch` blocks. A single Python file often becomes multiple Java files organized by class hierarchy.

diffs.from: Functions are first-class. No class wrapper needed for utilities.
diffs.to: All code must live in a class. Static methods for utilities.
Static, nominal. Every parameter and return type required.

diffs.from: list, dict, set are built-in; no import needed.
diffs.to: ArrayList, HashMap, HashSet from java.util. Generics required.

diffs.from: All exceptions unchecked. No throws declaration.
diffs.to: Checked exceptions require throws or catch. Runtime exceptions are unchecked.

diffs.from: Dataclass or plain class. __init__ for initialization.
diffs.to: Full POJO: private fields, constructor, getters, setters, equals, hashCode.

examples.title: Module-level function → static method in a class
examples.description: Python top-level functions must be wrapped in a Java class as static methods.
examples.fromCode:
def is_palindrome(s: str) -> bool:
    cleaned = s.lower().replace(" ", "")
    return cleaned == cleaned[::-1]

print(is_palindrome("A man a plan a canal Panama"))

examples.title: Python list operations → ArrayList
examples.description: Python's built-in list methods have direct ArrayList equivalents.
examples.fromCode:
items = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]
items.append("date")
items.remove("banana")
filtered = [x for x in items if len(x) > 5]
print(filtered)  # ['banana' removed, so: ['cherry', 'date']... ]

tips.title: Every function needs a class
tips.body: Java has no module-level functions. Wrap utilities in a class with static methods, or create proper object-oriented classes with instance methods.

tips.title: list → ArrayList, dict → HashMap
tips.body: Python list becomes ArrayList<T>. Python dict[K]V becomes HashMap<K,V>. All collection operations need java.util imports and generic type parameters.

tips.title: Optional hints → required types
tips.body: Python type hints become mandatory Java declarations. Every method parameter needs a type. Return void explicitly when there is no return value.

tips.title: Dataclass → Java record (Java 16+)
tips.body: Python dataclasses map cleanly to Java records: record Person(String name, int age) {} generates constructor, getters, equals, hashCode automatically.
